| license-type | License | read, write | The license of the program, as a value of the %GtkLicense enumeration. The #GtkAboutDialog will automatically fill out a standard disclaimer and li... |

| logo-icon-name | utf8 | read, write | A named icon to use as the logo for the about box. This property overrides the #GtkAboutDialog:logo property. |
| program-name | utf8 | read, write | The name of the program. If this is not set, it defaults to g_get_application_name(). |

| version | utf8 | read, write | The version of the program. |
| website | utf8 | read, write | The URL for the link to the website of the program. This should be a string starting with "http://. |
| website-label | utf8 | read, write | The label for the link to the website of the program. |
| wrap-license | gboolean | read, write | Whether to wrap the text in the license dialog. |

| Name | Type | Access | Notes |

| detail-width-chars | gint | read, write | Width of a detail cell, in characters. A value of 0 allows any width. See gtk_calendar_set_detail_func(). |
| month | gint | read, write | The selected month (as a number between 0 and 11). This property gets initially set to the current month. |
| no-month-change | gboolean | read, write | Determines whether the selected month can be changed. |

| show-heading | gboolean | read, write | Determines whether a heading is displayed. |
| show-week-numbers | gboolean | read, write | Determines whether week numbers are displayed. |
| year | gint | read, write | The selected year. This property gets initially set to the current year. |

| month-changed | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when the user clicks a button to change the selected month on a calendar. |
| next-month | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when the user switched to the next month. |
| next-year | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when user switched to the next year. |
| prev-month | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when the user switched to the previous month. |
| prev-year | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when user switched to the previous year. |

| tearoff-title | utf8 | read, write | A title that may be displayed by the window manager when the popup is torn-off. |
| wrap-width | gint | read, write | If wrap-width is set to a positive value, items in the popup will be laid out along multiple columns, starting a new row on reaching the wrap width. |

| buffer | EntryBuffer | read, write |  |
| caps-lock-warning | gboolean | read, write | Whether password entries will show a warning when Caps Lock is on. Note that the warning is shown using a secondary icon, and thus does not work if... |

| editable | gboolean | read, write |  |
| enable-emoji-completion | gboolean | read, write |  |
| has-frame | gboolean | read, write |  |
| im-module | utf8 | read, write | Which IM (input method) module should be used for this entry. See #GtkIMContext. Setting this to a non-%NULL value overrides the system-wide IM mod... |
| inner-border | Border | read, write | Sets the text area's border between the text and the frame. |
| input-hints | InputHints | read, write | Additional hints (beyond #GtkEntry:input-purpose) that allow input methods to fine-tune their behaviour. |
| input-purpose | InputPurpose | read, write | The purpose of this text field. This property can be used by on-screen keyboards and other input methods to adjust their behaviour. Note that setti... |
| invisible-char | guint | read, write | The invisible character is used when masking entry contents (in \"password mode\")"). When it is not explicitly set with the #GtkEntry:invisible-ch... |
| invisible-char-set | gboolean | read, write | Whether the invisible char has been set for the #GtkEntry. |
| max-length | gint | read, write |  |
| max-width-chars | gint | read, write | The desired maximum width of the entry, in characters. If this property is set to -1, the width will be calculated automatically. |
| overwrite-mode | gboolean | read, write | If text is overwritten when typing in the #GtkEntry. |
| placeholder-text | utf8 | read, write | The text that will be displayed in the #GtkEntry when it is empty and unfocused. |
| populate-all | gboolean | read, write | If :populate-all is %TRUE, the #GtkEntry::populate-popup signal is also emitted for touch popups. |
| primary-icon-activatable | gboolean | read, write | Whether the primary icon is activatable. GTK+ emits the #GtkEntry::icon-press and #GtkEntry::icon-release signals only on sensitive, activatable ic... |

| primary-icon-name | utf8 | read, write | The icon name to use for the primary icon for the entry. |
| primary-icon-pixbuf | GdkPixbuf.Pixbuf | read, write | A pixbuf to use as the primary icon for the entry. |

| primary-icon-stock | utf8 | read, write | The stock id to use for the primary icon for the entry. |
| primary-icon-storage-type | ImageType | read | The representation which is used for the primary icon of the entry. |

| primary-icon-tooltip-text | utf8 | read, write | The contents of the tooltip on the primary icon. Also see gtk_entry_set_icon_tooltip_text(). |
| progress-fraction | gdouble | read, write | The current fraction of the task that's been completed. |
| progress-pulse-step | gdouble | read, write | The fraction of total entry width to move the progress bouncing block for each call to gtk_entry_progress_pulse(). |

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| copy-clipboard | `` | none | last |  | The ::copy-clipboard signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted to copy the selection to the clipboard. The default bindi... |
| cut-clipboard | `` | none | last |  | The ::cut-clipboard signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted to cut the selection to the clipboard. The default binding... |
| delete-from-cursor | `type: DeleteType, count: gint` | none | last |  | The ::delete-from-cursor signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted when the user initiates a text deletion. If the @type... |
| icon-press | `icon_pos: EntryIconPosition, event: Gdk.Event` | none | last | 2.16 | The ::icon-press signal is emitted when an activatable icon is clicked. |
| icon-release | `icon_pos: EntryIconPosition, event: Gdk.Event` | none | last | 2.16 | The ::icon-release signal is emitted on the button release from a mouse click over an activatable icon. |

| move-cursor | `step: MovementStep, count: gint, extend_selection: gboolean` | none | last |  | The ::move-cursor signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted when the user initiates a cursor movement. If the cursor is ... |
| paste-clipboard | `` | none | last |  | The ::paste-clipboard signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted to paste the contents of the clipboard into the text vie... |
| populate-popup | `widget: Widget` | none | last |  | The ::populate-popup signal gets emitted before showing the context menu of the entry. If you need to add items to the context menu, connect to thi... |
| preedit-changed | `preedit: utf8` | none | last | 2.20 | If an input method is used, the typed text will not immediately be committed to the buffer. So if you are interested in the text, connect to this s... |
| toggle-overwrite | `` | none | last |  | The ::toggle-overwrite signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted to toggle the overwrite mode of the entry. The default ... |

#### Virtual Methods

These are the names to use from `sqgi.register_class({ overrides = ... })` when SQGI can override them.

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activate | `activate() -> none` |  |  | Class handler for the #GtkEntry::activate signal. The default implementation calls gtk_window_activate_default() on the entry???s top-level window. |
| backspace | `backspace() -> none` |  |  | Class handler for the #GtkEntry::backspace signal. The default implementation deletes the selection or a single character or word. |
| copy_clipboard | `copy_clipboard() -> none` |  |  | Class handler for the #GtkEntry::copy-clipboard signal. The default implementation copies the selection, if one exists. |
| cut_clipboard | `cut_clipboard() -> none` |  |  | Class handler for the #GtkEntry::cut-clipboard signal. The default implementation cuts the selection, if one exists. |

| update_custom_widget | `update_custom_widget(widget: Widget, setup: PageSetup, settings: PrintSettings) -> none` |  |  | Emitted after change of selected printer. |

#### Properties

| Name | Type | Access | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| copy-target-list | TargetList | read | The list of targets this buffer supports for clipboard copying and as DND source. |
| cursor-position | gint | read | The position of the insert mark (as offset from the beginning of the buffer). It is useful for getting notified when the cursor moves. |
| has-selection | gboolean | read | Whether the buffer has some text currently selected. |
| paste-target-list | TargetList | read | The list of targets this buffer supports for clipboard pasting and as DND destination. |
| tag-table | TextTagTable | read, write, construct-only |  |
| text | utf8 | read, write | The text content of the buffer. Without child widgets and images, see gtk_text_buffer_get_text() for more information. |

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| apply-tag | `tag: TextTag, start: TextIter, end: TextIter` | none | last |  | The ::apply-tag signal is emitted to apply a tag to a range of text in a #GtkTextBuffer. Applying actually occurs in the default handler. Note that... |
| begin-user-action | `` | none | last |  | The ::begin-user-action signal is emitted at the beginning of a single user-visible operation on a #GtkTextBuffer. See also: gtk_text_buffer_begin_... |
| changed | `` | none | last |  | The ::changed signal is emitted when the content of a #GtkTextBuffer has changed. |
| delete-range | `start: TextIter, end: TextIter` | none | last |  | The ::delete-range signal is emitted to delete a range from a #GtkTextBuffer. Note that if your handler runs before the default handler it must not... |
| end-user-action | `` | none | last |  | The ::end-user-action signal is emitted at the end of a single user-visible operation on the #GtkTextBuffer. See also: gtk_text_buffer_end_user_act... |
| insert-child-anchor | `location: TextIter, anchor: TextChildAnchor` | none | last |  | The ::insert-child-anchor signal is emitted to insert a #GtkTextChildAnchor in a #GtkTextBuffer. Insertion actually occurs in the default handler. ... |
| insert-pixbuf | `location: TextIter, pixbuf: GdkPixbuf.Pixbuf` | none | last |  | The ::insert-pixbuf signal is emitted to insert a #GdkPixbuf in a #GtkTextBuffer. Insertion actually occurs in the default handler. Note that if yo... |
| insert-text | `location: TextIter, text: utf8, len: gint` | none | last |  | The ::insert-text signal is emitted to insert text in a #GtkTextBuffer. Insertion actually occurs in the default handler. Note that if your handler... |
| mark-deleted | `mark: TextMark` | none | last |  | The ::mark-deleted signal is emitted as notification after a #GtkTextMark is deleted. See also: gtk_text_buffer_delete_mark(). |

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| columns-changed | `` | none | last |  | The number of columns of the treeview has changed. |
| cursor-changed | `` | none | last |  | The position of the cursor (focused cell) has changed. |
| expand-collapse-cursor-row | `object: gboolean, p0: gboolean, p1: gboolean` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| move-cursor | `step: MovementStep, direction: gint` | gboolean | last |  | The #GtkTreeView::move-cursor signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted when the user presses one of the cursor keys. Ap... |
| row-activated | `path: TreePath, column: TreeViewColumn` | none | last |  | The "row-activated" signal is emitted when the method gtk_tree_view_row_activated() is called, when the user double clicks a treeview row with the ... |
| row-collapsed | `iter: TreeIter, path: TreePath` | none | last |  | The given row has been collapsed (child nodes are hidden). |
| row-expanded | `iter: TreeIter, path: TreePath` | none | last |  | The given row has been expanded (child nodes are shown). |
| select-all | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| select-cursor-parent | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| select-cursor-row | `object: gboolean` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| start-interactive-search | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| test-collapse-row | `iter: TreeIter, path: TreePath` | gboolean | last |  | The given row is about to be collapsed (hide its children nodes). Use this signal if you need to control the collapsibility of individual rows. |
| test-expand-row | `iter: TreeIter, path: TreePath` | gboolean | last |  | The given row is about to be expanded (show its children nodes). Use this signal if you need to control the expandability of individual rows. |
| toggle-cursor-row | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| unselect-all | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |

### Gtk.TreeViewColumn

Parent: `GObject.InitiallyUnowned` ?? Implements: `Buildable`, `CellLayout` ?? GType: `GtkTreeViewColumn` ?? C type: `GtkTreeViewColumn`

### Gtk.Window

Parent: `Bin` ?? Subclasses: `ApplicationWindow`, `Assistant`, `Dialog`, `OffscreenWindow`, `Plug`, `ShortcutsWindow` ?? Implements: `Atk.ImplementorIface`, `Buildable` ?? GType: `GtkWindow` ?? C type: `GtkWindow`

A GtkWindow is a toplevel window which can contain other widgets. Windows normally have decorations that are under the control of the windowing sys...

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activate-default | `` | none | last |  | The ::activate-default signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted when the user activates the default widget of @window. |
| activate-focus | `` | none | last |  | The ::activate-focus signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted when the user activates the currently focused widget of @... |
| enable-debugging | `toggle: gboolean` | gboolean | last |  | The ::enable-debugging signal is a [keybinding signal][GtkBindingSignal] which gets emitted when the user enables or disables interactive debugging... |
| keys-changed | `` | none | first |  | The ::keys-changed signal gets emitted when the set of accelerators or mnemonics that are associated with @window changes. |
| set-focus | `widget: Widget?` | none | last | 2.24 | This signal is emitted whenever the currently focused widget in this window changes. |

### Gtk.WindowGroup

Parent: `GObject.Object` ?? GType: `GtkWindowGroup` ?? C type: `GtkWindowGroup`

A #GtkWindowGroup restricts the effect of grabs to windows in the same group, thereby making window groups almost behave like separate applications...

#### Fields

| Field | Type |
| --- | --- |
| parent_instance | GObject.Object |
| priv | WindowGroupPrivate |

#### Constructors

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| new | `WindowGroup.new() -> WindowGroup` | gtk_window_group_new |  | Creates a new #GtkWindowGroup object. Grabs added with gtk_grab_add() only affect windows within the same #GtkWindowGroup. |

#### Methods

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| add_window | `WindowGroup.add_window(window: Window) -> none` | gtk_window_group_add_window |  | Adds a window to a #GtkWindowGroup. |
| get_current_device_grab | `WindowGroup.get_current_device_grab(device: Gdk.Device) -> Widget` | gtk_window_group_get_current_device_grab | 3.0 | Returns the current grab widget for @device, or %NULL if none. |
| get_current_grab | `WindowGroup.get_current_grab() -> Widget` | gtk_window_group_get_current_grab | 2.22 | Gets the current grab widget of the given group, see gtk_grab_add(). |
| list_windows | `WindowGroup.list_windows() -> GLib.List` | gtk_window_group_list_windows | 2.14 | Returns a list of the #GtkWindows that belong to @window_group. |
| remove_window | `WindowGroup.remove_window(window: Window) -> none` | gtk_window_group_remove_window |  | Removes a window from a #GtkWindowGroup. |

## Enums

### Gtk.Align

GType: `GtkAlign` ?? C type: `GtkAlign`

Controls how a widget deals with extra space in a single (x or y) dimension. Alignment only matters if the widget receives a ???too large??? alloca...

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| baseline | 4 | baseline | align the widget according to the baseline. Since 3.10. |
| center | 3 | center | center natural width of widget inside the allocation |
| end | 2 | end | snap to right or bottom side, leaving space on left or top |
| fill | 0 | fill | stretch to fill all space if possible, center if no meaningful way to stretch |
| start | 1 | start | snap to left or top side, leaving space on right or bottom |

### Gtk.ArrowPlacement

GType: `GtkArrowPlacement` ?? C type: `GtkArrowPlacement`

Used to specify the placement of scroll arrows in scrolling menus.

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| both | 0 | both | Place one arrow on each end of the menu. |
| end | 2 | end | Place both arrows at the bottom of the menu. |
| start | 1 | start | Place both arrows at the top of the menu. |

### Gtk.ArrowType

GType: `GtkArrowType` ?? C type: `GtkArrowType`

Used to indicate the direction in which an arrow should point.

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| down | 1 | down | Represents a downward pointing arrow. |
| left | 2 | left | Represents a left pointing arrow. |
| none | 4 | none | No arrow. Since 2.10. |
| right | 3 | right | Represents a right pointing arrow. |
| up | 0 | up | Represents an upward pointing arrow. |

### Gtk.AssistantPageType

GType: `GtkAssistantPageType` ?? C type: `GtkAssistantPageType`

An enum for determining the page role inside the #GtkAssistant. It's used to handle buttons sensitivity and visibility. Note that an assistant need...

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| confirm | 2 | confirm | The page lets the user confirm or deny the changes. The Back and Apply buttons will be shown. |
| content | 0 | content | The page has regular contents. Both the Back and forward buttons will be shown. |
| custom | 5 | custom | Used for when other page types are not appropriate. No buttons will be shown, and the application must add its own buttons through gtk_assistant_ad... |
| intro | 1 | intro | The page contains an introduction to the assistant task. Only the Forward button will be shown if there is a next page. |
| progress | 4 | progress | Used for tasks that take a long time to complete, blocks the assistant until the page is marked as complete. Only the back button will be shown. |
| summary | 3 | summary | The page informs the user of the changes done. Only the Close button will be shown. |

### Gtk.PolicyType

GType: `GtkPolicyType` ?? C type: `GtkPolicyType`

Determines how the size should be computed to achieve the one of the visibility mode for the scrollbars.

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| always | 0 | always | The scrollbar is always visible. The view size is independent of the content. |
| automatic | 1 | automatic | The scrollbar will appear and disappear as necessary. For example, when all of a #GtkTreeView can not be seen. |
| external | 3 | external | Don't show a scrollbar, but don't force the size to follow the content. This can be used e.g. to make multiple scrolled windows share a scrollbar. ... |
| never | 2 | never | The scrollbar should never appear. In this mode the content determines the size. |

GType: `GtkPrintOperationAction` ?? C type: `GtkPrintOperationAction`

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| export | 3 | export | Export to a file. This requires the export-filename property to be set. |
| preview | 2 | preview | Show the print preview. |
| print | 1 | print | Start to print without showing the print dialog, based on the current print settings. |
| print_dialog | 0 | print-dialog | Show the print dialog. |

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |

### Gtk.PrintStatus

GType: `GtkPrintStatus` ?? C type: `GtkPrintStatus`

The status gives a rough indication of the completion of a running print operation.

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| finished | 7 | finished | The printing has been completed successfully. |
| finished_aborted | 8 | finished-aborted | The printing has been aborted. |
| generating_data | 2 | generating-data | This status is set while the pages are being rendered. |
| initial | 0 | initial | The printing has not started yet; this status is set initially, and while the print dialog is shown. |
| pending | 4 | pending | The print job has been sent to the printer, but is not printed for some reason, e.g. the printer may be stopped. |
| pending_issue | 5 | pending-issue | Some problem has occurred during printing, e.g. a paper jam. |
| preparing | 1 | preparing | This status is set while the begin-print signal is emitted and during pagination. |
| printing | 6 | printing | The printer is processing the print job. |
| sending_data | 3 | sending-data | The print job is being sent off to the printer. |

| target | 3 | target | Events are delivered in the default widget event handlers, note that widget implementations must chain up on button, motion, touch and grab broken ... |

### Gtk.StackTransitionType

GType: `GtkStackTransitionType` ?? C type: `GtkStackTransitionType`

These enumeration values describe the possible transitions between pages in a #GtkStack widget. New values may be added to this enumeration over time.

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| crossfade | 1 | crossfade | A cross-fade |
| none | 0 | none | No transition |
| over_down | 9 | over-down | Cover the old page by sliding down. Since: 3.12 |
| over_down_up | 17 | over-down-up | Cover the old page sliding down or uncover the new page sliding up, according to order. Since: 3.14 |
| over_left | 10 | over-left | Cover the old page by sliding to the left. Since: 3.12 |
| over_left_right | 18 | over-left-right | Cover the old page sliding left or uncover the new page sliding right, according to order. Since: 3.14 |
| over_right | 11 | over-right | Cover the old page by sliding to the right. Since: 3.12 |
| over_right_left | 19 | over-right-left | Cover the old page sliding right or uncover the new page sliding left, according to order. Since: 3.14 |
| over_up | 8 | over-up | Cover the old page by sliding up. Since 3.12 |
| over_up_down | 16 | over-up-down | Cover the old page sliding up or uncover the new page sliding down, according to order. Since: 3.12 |
| slide_down | 5 | slide-down | Slide from top down |
| slide_left | 3 | slide-left | Slide from right to left |
| slide_left_right | 6 | slide-left-right | Slide from left or right according to the children order |
| slide_right | 2 | slide-right | Slide from left to right |
| slide_up | 4 | slide-up | Slide from bottom up |
| slide_up_down | 7 | slide-up-down | Slide from top down or bottom up according to the order |
| under_down | 13 | under-down | Uncover the new page by sliding down. Since: 3.12 |
| under_left | 14 | under-left | Uncover the new page by sliding to the left. Since: 3.12 |
| under_right | 15 | under-right | Uncover the new page by sliding to the right. Since: 3.12 |
| under_up | 12 | under-up | Uncover the new page by sliding up. Since 3.12 |

### Gtk.CssSection

GType: `GtkCssSection` ?? C type: `GtkCssSection`

Defines a part of a CSS document. Because sections are nested into one another, you can use gtk_css_section_get_parent() to get the containing region.

#### Methods

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| get_end_line | `CssSection.get_end_line() -> guint` | gtk_css_section_get_end_line | 3.2 | Returns the line in the CSS document where this section end. The line number is 0-indexed, so the first line of the document will return 0. This va... |
| get_end_position | `CssSection.get_end_position() -> guint` | gtk_css_section_get_end_position | 3.2 | Returns the offset in bytes from the start of the current line returned via gtk_css_section_get_end_line(). This value may change in future invocat... |
| get_file | `CssSection.get_file() -> Gio.File` | gtk_css_section_get_file | 3.2 | Gets the file that @section was parsed from. If no such file exists, for example because the CSS was loaded via @gtk_css_provider_load_from_data(),... |
| get_parent | `CssSection.get_parent() -> CssSection` | gtk_css_section_get_parent | 3.2 | Gets the parent section for the given @section. The parent section is the section that contains this @section. A special case are sections of type ... |
| get_section_type | `CssSection.get_section_type() -> CssSectionType` | gtk_css_section_get_section_type | 3.2 | Gets the type of information that @section describes. |
| get_start_line | `CssSection.get_start_line() -> guint` | gtk_css_section_get_start_line | 3.2 | Returns the line in the CSS document where this section starts. The line number is 0-indexed, so the first line of the document will return 0. |
| get_start_position | `CssSection.get_start_position() -> guint` | gtk_css_section_get_start_position | 3.2 | Returns the offset in bytes from the start of the current line returned via gtk_css_section_get_start_line(). |
| ref | `CssSection.ref() -> CssSection` | gtk_css_section_ref | 3.2 | Increments the reference count on @section. |
| unref | `CssSection.unref() -> none` | gtk_css_section_unref | 3.2 | Decrements the reference count on @section, freeing the structure if the reference count reaches 0. |

### Gtk.PaperSize

GType: `GtkPaperSize` ?? C type: `GtkPaperSize`

GtkPaperSize handles paper sizes. It uses the standard called PWG 5101.1-2002 PWG: Standard for Media Standardized Names to name the paper sizes (a...

#### Constructors

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| new | `PaperSize.new(name: utf8?) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new | 2.10 | Creates a new #GtkPaperSize object by parsing a PWG 5101.1-2002 paper name. If @name is %NULL, the default paper size is returned, see gtk_paper_si... |
| new_custom | `PaperSize.new_custom(name: utf8, display_name: utf8, width: gdouble, height: gdouble, unit: Unit) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new_custom | 2.10 | Creates a new #GtkPaperSize object with the given parameters. |
| new_from_gvariant | `PaperSize.new_from_gvariant(variant: GLib.Variant) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new_from_gvariant | 3.22 | Deserialize a paper size from an a{sv} variant in the format produced by gtk_paper_size_to_gvariant(). |
| new_from_ipp | `PaperSize.new_from_ipp(ipp_name: utf8, width: gdouble, height: gdouble) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new_from_ipp | 3.16 | Creates a new #GtkPaperSize object by using IPP information. If @ipp_name is not a recognized paper name, @width and @height are used to construct ... |
| new_from_key_file | `PaperSize.new_from_key_file(key_file: GLib.KeyFile, group_name: utf8?) -> PaperSize throws` | gtk_paper_size_new_from_key_file | 2.12 | Reads a paper size from the group @group_name in the key file @key_file. |
| new_from_ppd | `PaperSize.new_from_ppd(ppd_name: utf8, ppd_display_name: utf8, width: gdouble, height: gdouble) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new_from_ppd | 2.10 | Creates a new #GtkPaperSize object by using PPD information. If @ppd_name is not a recognized PPD paper name, @ppd_display_name, @width and @height... |

#### Static Functions

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| get_default | `get_default() -> utf8` | gtk_paper_size_get_default | 2.10 | Returns the name of the default paper size, which depends on the current locale. |
| get_paper_sizes | `get_paper_sizes(include_custom: gboolean) -> GLib.List` | gtk_paper_size_get_paper_sizes | 2.12 | Creates a list of known paper sizes. |

#### Methods

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| copy | `PaperSize.copy() -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_copy | 2.10 | Copies an existing #GtkPaperSize. |
| free | `PaperSize.free() -> none` | gtk_paper_size_free | 2.10 | Free the given #GtkPaperSize object. |
| get_default_bottom_margin | `PaperSize.get_default_bottom_margin(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_default_bottom_margin | 2.10 | Gets the default bottom margin for the #GtkPaperSize. |
| get_default_left_margin | `PaperSize.get_default_left_margin(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_default_left_margin | 2.10 | Gets the default left margin for the #GtkPaperSize. |
| get_default_right_margin | `PaperSize.get_default_right_margin(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_default_right_margin | 2.10 | Gets the default right margin for the #GtkPaperSize. |
| get_default_top_margin | `PaperSize.get_default_top_margin(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_default_top_margin | 2.10 | Gets the default top margin for the #GtkPaperSize. |
| get_display_name | `PaperSize.get_display_name() -> utf8` | gtk_paper_size_get_display_name | 2.10 | Gets the human-readable name of the #GtkPaperSize. |
| get_height | `PaperSize.get_height(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_height | 2.10 | Gets the paper height of the #GtkPaperSize, in units of @unit. |
| get_name | `PaperSize.get_name() -> utf8` | gtk_paper_size_get_name | 2.10 | Gets the name of the #GtkPaperSize. |
| get_ppd_name | `PaperSize.get_ppd_name() -> utf8` | gtk_paper_size_get_ppd_name | 2.10 | Gets the PPD name of the #GtkPaperSize, which may be %NULL. |
| get_width | `PaperSize.get_width(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_width | 2.10 | Gets the paper width of the #GtkPaperSize, in units of @unit. |
| is_custom | `PaperSize.is_custom() -> gboolean` | gtk_paper_size_is_custom |  | Returns %TRUE if @size is not a standard paper size. |
| is_equal | `PaperSize.is_equal(size2: PaperSize) -> gboolean` | gtk_paper_size_is_equal | 2.10 | Compares two #GtkPaperSize objects. |
| is_ipp | `PaperSize.is_ipp() -> gboolean` | gtk_paper_size_is_ipp |  | Returns %TRUE if @size is an IPP standard paper size. |
| set_size | `PaperSize.set_size(width: gdouble, height: gdouble, unit: Unit) -> none` | gtk_paper_size_set_size | 2.10 | Changes the dimensions of a @size to @width x @height. |
| to_gvariant | `PaperSize.to_gvariant() -> GLib.Variant` | gtk_paper_size_to_gvariant | 3.22 | Serialize a paper size to an a{sv} variant. |
| to_key_file | `PaperSize.to_key_file(key_file: GLib.KeyFile, group_name: utf8) -> none` | gtk_paper_size_to_key_file | 2.12 | This function adds the paper size from @size to @key_file. |

If the command line contains any escape characters defin... |
| get_applications | `RecentInfo.get_applications(length: out gsize?) -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_get_applications | 2.10 | Retrieves the list of applications that have registered this resource. |
| get_description | `RecentInfo.get_description() -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_get_description | 2.10 | Gets the (short) description of the resource. |
| get_display_name | `RecentInfo.get_display_name() -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_get_display_name | 2.10 | Gets the name of the resource. If none has been defined, the basename of the resource is obtained. |
| get_gicon | `RecentInfo.get_gicon() -> Gio.Icon` | gtk_recent_info_get_gicon | 2.22 | Retrieves the icon associated to the resource MIME type. |
| get_groups | `RecentInfo.get_groups(length: out gsize?) -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_get_groups | 2.10 | Returns all groups registered for the recently used item @info. The array of returned group names will be %NULL terminated, so length might optiona... |
| get_icon | `RecentInfo.get_icon(size: gint) -> GdkPixbuf.Pixbuf` | gtk_recent_info_get_icon | 2.10 | Retrieves the icon of size @size associated to the resource MIME type. |
| get_mime_type | `RecentInfo.get_mime_type() -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_get_mime_type | 2.10 | Gets the MIME type of the resource. |
| get_modified | `RecentInfo.get_modified() -> time_t` | gtk_recent_info_get_modified | 2.10 | Gets the timestamp (seconds from system???s Epoch) when the meta-data for the resource was last modified. |
| get_private_hint | `RecentInfo.get_private_hint() -> gboolean` | gtk_recent_info_get_private_hint | 2.10 | Gets the value of the ???private??? flag. Resources in the recently used list that have this flag set to %TRUE should only be displayed by the appl... |
| get_short_name | `RecentInfo.get_short_name() -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_get_short_name | 2.10 | Computes a valid UTF-8 string that can be used as the name of the item in a menu or list. For example, calling this function on an item that refers... |
| get_uri | `RecentInfo.get_uri() -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_get_uri | 2.10 | Gets the URI of the resource. |
| get_uri_display | `RecentInfo.get_uri_display() -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_get_uri_display | 2.10 | Gets a displayable version of the resource???s URI. If the resource is local, it returns a local path; if the resource is not local, it returns the... |
| get_visited | `RecentInfo.get_visited() -> time_t` | gtk_recent_info_get_visited | 2.10 | Gets the timestamp (seconds from system???s Epoch) when the meta-data for the resource was last visited. |
| has_application | `RecentInfo.has_application(app_name: utf8) -> gboolean` | gtk_recent_info_has_application | 2.10 | Checks whether an application registered this resource using @app_name. |
| has_group | `RecentInfo.has_group(group_name: utf8) -> gboolean` | gtk_recent_info_has_group | 2.10 | Checks whether @group_name appears inside the groups registered for the recently used item @info. |
| is_local | `RecentInfo.is_local() -> gboolean` | gtk_recent_info_is_local | 2.10 | Checks whether the resource is local or not by looking at the scheme of its URI. |
| last_application | `RecentInfo.last_application() -> utf8` | gtk_recent_info_last_application | 2.10 | Gets the name of the last application that have registered the recently used resource represented by @info. |
| match | `RecentInfo.match(info_b: RecentInfo) -> gboolean` | gtk_recent_info_match | 2.10 | Checks whether two #GtkRecentInfo-struct point to the same resource. |
| ref | `RecentInfo.ref() -> RecentInfo` | gtk_recent_info_ref | 2.10 | Increases the reference count of @recent_info by one. |
| unref | `RecentInfo.unref() -> none` | gtk_recent_info_unref | 2.10 | Decreases the reference count of @info by one. If the reference count reaches zero, @info is deallocated, and the memory freed. |

| down | `TreePath.down() -> none` | gtk_tree_path_down |  | Moves @path to point to the first child of the current path. |
| free | `TreePath.free() -> none` | gtk_tree_path_free |  | Frees @path. If @path is %NULL, it simply returns. |
| get_depth | `TreePath.get_depth() -> gint` | gtk_tree_path_get_depth |  | Returns the current depth of @path. |

| prepend_index | `TreePath.prepend_index(index_: gint) -> none` | gtk_tree_path_prepend_index |  | Prepends a new index to a path. As a result, the depth of the path is increased. |
| prev | `TreePath.prev() -> gboolean` | gtk_tree_path_prev |  | Moves the @path to point to the previous node at the current depth, if it exists. |

| up | `TreePath.up() -> gboolean` | gtk_tree_path_up |  | Moves the @path to point to its parent node, if it has a parent. |
